label: eliza-1-9b
notes:
2x H100 / H200 with FP8 weight quant + FP8 KV. vLLM compiles FP8 W8A8 from the bf16 base repo (`elizalabs/eliza-1-9b`) at load time on Hopper, no separate fp8 repo needed.

--calculate-kv-scales is intentionally omitted: the eliza-1-9b base is Eliza-1 hybrid attention, and per vllm#37554 calibrated FP8 KV scales corrupt the GDN recurrent state under prefix caching because GDN layers have uninitialized `conv_state`/`ssm_state` during the dummy calibration pass. Default scales (1.0) work because typical bf16 KV activations stay within FP8 E4M3's ±448 range.

KV dtype is fp8_e4m3, not turboquant_*_nc, even though PR #38479 (turboquant) merged in v0.20.1: per upstream omlx#825 + the vLLM #38171 RFC discussion, hybrid GDN+attention models hit 'incompressible recurrent state' that limits TQ's compression and can interact badly with prefix caching on tool-calling traffic. Revisit after an A/B regression on this base.

WORKER CONTRACT: vLLM on port 8000. Deploy with the sibling `onstart-vllm.sh` (declared via `onstart_script`); it builds the vllm argv from this manifest's `vllm_args`, tails for `Application startup complete`, and execs the upstream pyworker (`worker.py`) with LLAMA_SERVER_PORT=$VLLM_PORT so the heartbeat contract still works. The GGUF/llama-server path under ../ollama/ remains available for direct upstream pyworker use.
